element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dual-input system mirrors Xevious; required for ground threat coverage
Yellow bell introduces risk/reward shooting mechanic; must shoot clouds to reveal
First offensive power-up; obtained by continued shooting of revealed bell
Up to 5 speed levels stackable; increases navigation difficulty
Precursor to Gradius option system; creates image copies for firepower scaling
Mutual exclusion with green bell; protective vs offensive trade-off
Ground enemy drop; spreads damage pattern
Screen-clear mechanic; appears when pressure peaks
Progressive loss: side-clip loses arm, both arms lost = bomb disabled
Recovery window only appears once per life; second loss = permanent bomb loss
Vertical/horizontal alignment enables synchronized enhanced damage

typical_relief_triggers: Obtaining red bell (barrier grants temporary invulnerability window)
Green bell duplication reduces player exposure time
Safe power-up collection from clouds during brief pauses
Two-player synchronized attacks create damage peaks that thin enemy density
typical_tension_triggers: Power-up loss on death significantly extends difficulty recovery
Arm damage progression (side-clips eventually disable bombs)
Bell color randomization creates uncertainty in upgrades
Dense enemy waves requiring split focus between air and ground targets
High difficulty spike noted in reviews suggests escalating enemy density/speed
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Vertical shmup with wave-based tension cycles; power-up dependency creates high stakes on death; recovery windows tied to cloud collection and ambulance arrival events

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Floating clouds hide bells; shooting clouds is optional but high-reward exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Continued shooting of revealed bell changes color (4 transformation stages); mechanic not explicitly taught

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of dual input system (shoot/bomb) with basic yellow bells; establishes risk-reward of cloud shooting
difficulty_curve.mid_game: White, blue, green, and red bells introduce upgrade paths and trade-offs (green vs red mutual exclusion); arm damage system penalty deepens with accumulation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Star power-ups appear suggesting density peaks; high difficulty noted by reviewers; power-up loss upon death severely impedes progression
notable_spikes: Post-death recovery penalty (must rebuild power-up chain)
Double-arm loss (bombs disabled, ambulance is one-time repair only)
Arcade original noted as high difficulty; Famicom port reception suggests maintained challenge

notable_level_patterns.description: Simultaneous air and ground enemies requiring split input focus (shoot vs bomb); reflects Xevious influence
Floating clouds scattered in flight path; player must choose whether to spend bullets revealing/cycling bells or maintain firepower focus
Side-clip penalties accumulate toward bomb disabling; recovery window (ambulance) creates binary pressure state: safe or permanently degraded
Two-player mode enables vertical/horizontal alignment for enhanced attacks; adds spatial puzzle element to damage optimization
